patches.summary: Rewrite dividendsInWindow to walk rows and parse both single-object and array dividend payloads.
patches.reason: Greptile P2 flagged that `compare --range --include-divs` silently dropped array-shape dividend rows. Yahoo dividend data lands in the resources table either as a single object ({date, amount}) keyed by '<SYM>:<date>', or as an array ([{date, amount}, ...]) keyed by the bare symbol. The prior implementation used json_extract(data, '$.amount') which returns NULL on the array shape, so the total-return path zeroed dividends for symbols whose syncer wrote arrays. The fix mirrors sumDividendsForSymbol in portfolio_dividends.go, which already handled both shapes.
patches.validated_outcome: Both shapes now contribute to total-return computation; tests pass.

patches.summary: Document the default symbol source for `insiders-net-buying` in Short, Long, and the --watchlist flag description.
patches.reason: Greptile P2 flagged that the command silently falls back to querying `watchlist_members` across all watchlists when neither --all nor --watchlist is set. On a fresh install with no watchlists yet, this yields an empty set and the user sees 'hint: no symbols to scan' without knowing why. The flag-level help also implied 'all watched symbols' was the default, which is technically true but misleading. Short, Long, and the flag description now spell out the three selection modes and the empty-watchlists edge case.
patches.validated_outcome: Behavior unchanged; only help text updated.

patches.summary: Open sql command's SQLite connection in read-only mode so CTE-wrapped writes are rejected at the driver layer.
patches.reason: Greptile P1 (security) flagged that the keyword-prefix DML blocklist could be bypassed with `WITH x AS (INSERT INTO ...) SELECT 1` — the head extracted from such a query is 'WITH X AS', which matches none of the banned prefixes. Because the `sql` command is annotated mcp:read-only, an MCP agent advertising this tool as non-destructive could be tricked into mutating the local store. Fix: open the connection via store.OpenReadOnly() which uses mode=ro at the driver, so writes are rejected regardless of how the SQL is shaped. The keyword prefix gate is kept only as a friendlier error for the common direct cases.
patches.validated_outcome: Behavior tests: 'SELECT name FROM sqlite_master' succeeds; 'DROP TABLE x' rejected with the friendly DML error; 'WITH x AS (INSERT INTO watchlists(name) VALUES(...)) SELECT 1' rejected by the read-only driver.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: TOCTOU window in cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter.Wait() allows burst under concurrent sync workers.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Greptile P2 flagged that between the first Unlock and the second Lock in Wait(), N goroutines under `sync --concurrency N` can all read the same stale lastRequest, compute identical sleeps, and burst-fire requests. The lock guards reads and writes but not the decision between them. The file lives in internal/cliutil/, which is generator-reserved per cli-printing-press's Phase 4.95 review policy: 'Out of scope: internal/cliutil/ and internal/mcp/cobratree/. Any finding there is a machine bug; route to retro, do not patch in place.' Patching the printed CLI hides the machine bug from the next CLI; the correct fix is a token-bucket or time.Ticker-based gate in cli-printing-press's emitter template.
